TextENGNew: "——Come on, wake up, Natsumi.@k@vS04/37/310300157. We're at the hospital."
...I must have fallen asleep without realizing it. Chisato gently shook my shoulder from beside me, and I opened my eyes.
Rubbing the sleep from my eyes, I turned to look out the window. A large white building towered above the surrounding cityscape, standing bright against the blue sky.
That was Saeki Hospital — one of the most prominent general hospitals in the prefecture, and the hospital where Chisato's father served as director.
The traffic light at the intersection just before the hospital turned red, and the car glided to a smooth stop. A scattering of pedestrians hurried back and forth across the crosswalk in front of us.
"Feeling any better?"
"Yeah.@k@vS04/36/310100161. ...Oh, sorry.@k@vS04/36/310100162. I fell asleep while you were talking."
"It's fine, it's fine, don't worry about it.@k@vS04/37/310300160. You were pretty out of it from the moment you got in the car, Natsumi~."
"I-I wasn't that bad,@k@vS04/36/310100164. ...I don't think."
I wanted to argue, but I didn't have much confidence. ...My memory was hazy, but I vaguely recalled Chisato talking passionately about some popular new artist.
That said, I'd never been very interested in music, and on top of that, a wave of drowsiness had crashed over me all at once, so I'd apparently drifted right off.

"Well, my conversational skills clearly still need work.@k@vS04/37/310300162. A true master should be able to blow away even the listener's drowsiness and keep them hanging on every word.@k@vS04/37/310300163. Hmmmm..."
"...Chisato, what exactly are you aspiring to be?"
"Hmm?@k@vS04/37/310300165. How about a politician, for starters?"
"I-I don't even know how to respond to that..."

Even when she asked for my opinion, I was at a loss for words. ...I'll keep it to myself, but I nearly blurted out 'comedian' on reflex. Either way, it hardly seemed like an easy career to break into.
changeReason: OLD incorrectly says narrator expected Chisato to say comedian; JPN and NEW correctly state the narrator herself nearly blurted it out.

TextENGNew: "Heh heh heh...@k@vS04/37/310300167. But Natsumi, you really do have the cutest sleeping face~.@k@vS04/37/310300168. If you were that comfortable, want to ride back to school and make another round trip?"
"C-come on... don't tease me."
She grinned at me impishly, and I puffed out my cheeks, my face turning red.
...But honestly, the ride in Chisato's private car was something special. Sinking back into the plush seats with barely any vibration, drowsiness always overtook me in no time.
And the driver, Yamashina-san, was a true professional — smooth through every turn and stop.
I don't think Dad is a bad driver, but the feel was clearly different from our family car outings, so it must come down to the gap in practiced skill.

Before long, the light turned green, and the car pulled forward without a sound. After a short drive, we passed through the hospital's main gate, circled the roundabout, and headed toward the drop-off area.
"Oh, isn't the service entrance around back?@k@vS04/37/310300170. Should we go around that way?"
"No, it's fine.@k@vS04/36/310100169. There's a staff elevator right near the entrance."
"...Is that so?"
"Chisato... this is your dad's hospital, you know?"
"Nahahaha~!@k@vS04/37/310300173. ——Oh, Yamashina-san. You can stop here."
"Certainly."

Yamashina-san answered in his courteous tone, then brought the car to a stop at the shaded parking bollard by the building. Chisato and I thanked him, then opened our respective doors and stepped outside.
The summer sun blazed down upon the earth, and the trees and plants glistened with vibrant green, breathing in the fullness of the season.
The shrill chorus of cicadas echoed all around us, and my skin was enveloped in the heat, breaking out in a film of sweat almost instantly.
"Mmm~~, the outside air really is the best~!@k@vS04/37/310300175. Oh, my beloved world above ground~!"
"...You're exaggerating, Chisato."

A glance at my watch showed that just over twenty minutes had passed since we'd left school. ...I wasn't really in a position to comment, having been asleep the entire time, but it didn't seem like the kind of journey that warranted that level of relief.
"Well, cars just don't agree with me, you know?@k@vS04/37/310300177. I can't stand sitting still in a sealed-up space~."
"Ahaha, that's true.@k@vS04/36/310100173. You're more of the, well, physical type, Chisato."
"Hey now, at least say 'woman of action.'@k@|@y@vS04/37/310300179. ...Oh, how are we on time?"
"We're fine, still plenty of time.@k@vS04/36/310100175. Thanks for the ride — it was a huge help."
There were still nearly thirty minutes until my start time. When I took the train, I always arrived at the last possible second, so the difference was enormous.
"I wish I could drive you every week.@k@vS04/37/310300181. But this is my dad's car, so it's usually out on weekends~."
"No, even getting a ride once in a while makes me happy.@k@vS04/36/310100177. I'm not great with trains."
I said that with a shrug and stuck out my tongue. ...More accurately, it was the crowds of the city that I couldn't stand.

Hordes of people with no regard for anyone around them, faces fixed straight ahead, marching relentlessly toward their destinations. The station platform, overflowing with all of them. The stairs. The train cars.
changeReason: OLD drops the specific list of crowded places (platform, stairs, train car) present in JPN.

TextENGNew: Passing through the cold, sterile automatic ticket gates into the station plaza, brimming with noise. The intersections. The entertainment districts.
Chisato and the others, who had grown up with all of this as normal, surely couldn't understand.
But when I found myself face to face with this city life I'd once dreamed of... rather than embracing it with joy and hope, I felt my legs go weak with bewilderment.
Of course, when I was with Chisato and Tamako, I could enjoy shopping and wandering around while chatting about nothing in particular. It was fun.
...But the moment I found myself alone for whatever reason, I'd be seized by the illusion of having been thrown into another world, and a sudden, painful anxiety would tighten in my chest.
Walking through the crowded streets alone was... quite honestly, terrifying.
A cold, impersonal urban space where no one looks at you, no one speaks to you. It was as if I'd become a foreigner who couldn't speak the language... a gnawing sense of loneliness and not belonging.
When would this feeling of displacement ever go away...?
Or rather — would I ever truly be able to fit in here...?
"...Natsumi?"
"Oh, ——sorry."
Noticing Chisato's expression cloud over, I hurriedly waved my hands as if to brush it off.
changeReason: OLD reverses the subject: JPN says Natsumi noticed Chisato's expression cloud over; OLD says Chisato noticed Natsumi's.

TextENGNew: ...No good. Making my friends worry needlessly like this — that was exactly the kind of thing that proved I didn't belong in the city.
"Did something happen on the train, Natsumi?@k@vS04/37/310300184. If something's bothering you, you can talk to me about it."

"No, it's not that.@k@vS04/36/310100180. ...I still can't get through the automatic ticket gates smoothly.@k@vS04/36/310100181. The other day I put my ticket in properly, but the gate still stopped me halfway through. I guess I just have an aversion to them..."
"...Ahh, I see.@k@|@y@vS04/37/310300186. Ahahahaha!@k@vS04/37/310300187. Come to think of it, your face was beet red and you were practically on the verge of tears, weren't you~?"
"I-I was not about to cry~!"
"Oh yes you were — at the very least, your eyes were watery.@k@vS04/37/310300189. You were all like, 'Help me, Chisato, Tamako~!'@k@vS04/37/310300190. Heh heh heh!"
The suspicious look from a moment ago vanished, and Chisato laughed merrily.
...Even if it was at my expense, being included in the conversation was so much better. It gave me the reassurance of belonging to the group...
changeReason: OLD mistranslates; JPN says being included in conversation is reassuring; OLD renders as "the way we get there is always good."

TextENGNew: "——Chisato."
Then, suddenly.
An older gentleman approached us, wearing an expensive-looking suit with refined elegance. Chisato seemed to notice him too, tilting her head slightly as she turned around.
"Oh, Dad.@k@vS04/37/310300193. That was quick — are you done already?"
"Don't just ask 'are you done already?'@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1003. What was that mannish laugh just now?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1004. Can't you carry yourself a little more like a proper young lady?"
He gave Chisato a light tap on the head with an exasperated look. It shouldn't have hurt much, but Chisato recoiled with an exaggerated 'Ow!' and turned to face him with a resentful glare.
"Hey, what was that for?@k@vS04/37/310300195. I'm against violence and sexual harassment~!"
"...Don't wave women's rights around only when it's convenient for you.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1006. I've told you before — rights and obligations are two sides of the same coin."
"That's impossible.@k@vS04/37/310300197. I mean, I'm your daughter, after all."
"Use whatever you can, even your parents! As the saying goes.@k@vS04/37/310300199. And there's that lovely proverb, too — 'children grow up watching their parents' backs'~."
"Now, now — don't go saying things that give the wrong impression.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1008. I certainly don't recall raising you to be like this,@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1009. ——I think."
"...Then why are you looking away, dear father?"
"A-ahaha..."

Watching their exchange, I couldn't help but laugh despite my nervousness. He spoke like a perfect gentleman of reason and propriety, but Chisato and her father were practically twins in manner and temperament.
Like father, like daughter... or was it the other way around?
changeReason: OLD translates この子にしてこの親あり as "they're so backwards"; NEW correctly renders the parent-child resemblance proverb.

TextENGNew: "——Hmm?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1011. And who might you be?"
"Oh... I'm Natsumi Kimiyoshi.@k@vS04/36/310100185. I'm Chisato's classmate."

"Ah, so you're Natsumi-chan.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1013. My daughter has told me about you.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1014. ...Pleased to meet you. I'm Chisato's father.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1015. Thank you for looking after her."
"Oh, no, not at all..."
"That's backwards — I'm the one looking after her.@k@vS04/37/310300202. Natsumi is my adorable little pet, after all~."
She suddenly pulled me into a tight squeeze, and my face turned bright red. Ch-Chisato! I know this is normal for you, but please consider the time and place~!
"Good grief — the moment she sees something cute, she's at it again.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1017. ...Do you act this way at school, too?"
"Of course~, it's how I express my love.@k@vS04/37/310300204. And don't even think about asking for some yourself, Dad~. Hmhm~."
"Oh~?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_saekiF1019. This from the girl who couldn't even take proper care of her tropical fish."
...Um, am I being ranked alongside guppies here? And Chisato's father — please stop looking so amused and help me!
After that, I spent a good ten minutes or so squirming and struggling in Chisato's embrace, until her father finally glanced down at his watch...
changeReason: OLD says Natsumi looked at the watch; JPN says the father looked at his watch, triggering the scene's end.

TextENGNew: "Well then, Natsumi, see you later~!"
"...Haah, honestly...!"
I glared at Chisato as she waved cheerfully from the back seat window, while I tried to straighten out my mussed hair and disheveled collar.
...The one leaving was perfectly satisfied, which was all well and good, but I wished she'd spare a thought for me — left behind with all these curious stares boring into my back.
Hospital visitors, nurses, even the security guard — they had all peeked over to see what was going on as we squealed and carried on...
"Ah ha ha ha!@k@vS04/37/310300207. Natsumi was just too cute, so Chii-chan got a little carried away.@k@vS04/37/310300208. Forgive me, pretty please~."
"I feel like 'cute' is being used as a blanket excuse for everything here..."
"Hm?@k@vS04/37/310300210. Don't you like being called cute?@k@vS04/37/310300211. I really do mean it, you know~."
A deep sigh escaped me as I turned away.
Even if Chisato truly meant it and wasn't just flattering me... if the word 'cute' could excuse any transgression, then the wicked magistrates in period dramas wouldn't be so despised by women.
"——Oh, that reminds me, Natsumi. I forgot to mention.@k@vS04/37/310300213. Career counseling interviews start next week — you remember, right?"
"Huh?@k@vS04/36/310100194. Yeah, I remember... what about it?"
I blinked, not understanding what she was getting at. Seeing my blank expression, Chisato muttered 'I knew it' with a rueful half-smile.
changeReason: OLD drops Chisato's specific utterance やっぱりな ("I knew it") revealing her prior suspicion.

TextENGNew: "Tamako was worried about you.@k@vS04/37/310300215. I don't know how things were at your old school, but for us, this time in June is kind of a big deal."
"It affects which classes we get placed in next year, and it can determine whether we end up in summer supplementary courses.@k@vS04/37/310300217. ...Did you know that?"
I was dumbfounded, hearing something I hadn't expected at all.

"Yeah, looks like Tamako's hunch was right.@k@vS04/37/310300219. Just so you know, if you give sloppy answers, you'll definitely end up spending half your summer break in extra classes.@k@vS04/37/310300220. And they'll make you attend university info sessions too... You don't want that, right?"
"What?!@k@vS04/36/310100198. N-no, that sounds awful~!"

At my old school, before I could have my individual meeting with the teacher about career counseling, I'd already transferred. From what I'd heard, though, it was just simple questions like what you wanted to be when you grew up.
So I'd assumed it would be the same here, and hadn't paid any attention.

...But as things stood, I had absolutely no idea about schools to aim for or career paths — I didn't know what was where or what was what. Preparation and research? Naturally, I'd done none of it at all...
"Wh-what should I do...?"
"...Good grief."
Seeing my distress, Chisato's expression suddenly turned serious. She pulled out a planner and flipped through the pages, spoke briefly to her father sitting beside her, then turned back to me.
"In that case, Natsumi.@k@vS04/37/310300223. Are you free tomorrow?"
"...Huh, tomorrow?@k@vS04/36/310100201. I should be, but... why?"
"You know the library right near school, right?@k@vS04/37/310300225. Let's meet up there and have a strategy session."
"Your attendance number is near the beginning, so your turn will probably come early next week.@k@vS04/37/310300227. So we should get you prepared beforehand, okay?"
"Y-you'd really do that?"

"Of course!@k@vS04/37/310300229. ——Oh, I'll get in touch with Tamako too.@k@vS04/37/310300230. She's the class president, after all, so she knows all about the academic stuff. I'm sure she'll be a big help."
"But... it's a Sunday, and it'd be all because of me..."
"Ahaha, that's nothing at all.@k@vS04/37/310300232. In return, though, you're treating me to ice cream afterwards~."
Chisato said that with a playful wink and a smile.
...I felt terribly guilty for troubling them with my lack of preparation.
But without their advice, I'd surely stumble over whatever questions were thrown at me, and only make the teacher think worse of me. So I decided to gratefully accept their offer.
"...I'm sorry, Chisato.@k@vS04/36/310100205. I feel bad asking Tamako too, but... would you?"
"You got it!@k@vS04/37/310300234. I'll call you tonight.@k@vS04/37/310300235. Well then, good luck at work~!"
The car carrying Chisato pulled away with a gentle purr of the engine and headed out through the hospital gate. ...As it turned right, Chisato waved at me from the window, so I waved back.
Left alone, I slowly lowered my hand and let out another sigh. ...Not the drained kind from before. This one was heavier... tinged with a sinking feeling.
The road ahead. My future.
Given my year in school, it wasn't as though I'd been completely oblivious to the subject.

...It was just that for this entire past year, I'd been so consumed with keeping up with the flow around me and not getting left behind that I'd had no room to even consider what I might hope or choose for myself.
Mom would surely push for higher education.
She and Dad had told me before how they'd struggled at work because they'd graduated from a lesser-known university. So I was sure they expected me to get into a decent college.
And even I understood that would be the choice that would make them happiest.

But... honestly, there wasn't anything in particular I wanted to do, and I hadn't given a thought to building an academic record. And surely my abilities fell short, too. ...After all, I hadn't been in that kind of environment until now.
Playing happily with friends I could truly open up to.
School grades were like a carefree game — competing for scores as casually as keeping points in play.
Everyone had their own strengths and weaknesses.
Since hardly anyone excelled at everything, there was no sense of superiority or inferiority. It was easygoing.
That was who I was in Okinomiya. That was how all the kids my age lived there.
But... in this city, I was sure that kind of life wouldn't be tolerated. I couldn't remain in a world untouched by jealousy and rivalry. I'd find myself worrying about how others saw me.
No... it had already happened. And perhaps the day would come when I'd start feeling that way about Chisato and Tamako, too.
If that happened... would I still be able to stay close to them the way I was now...?
Suddenly, a sound like a school chime drifted down from the hospital rooftop. It was the signal announcing the end of consultation hours at Saeki Hospital.
And for me, it was an important signal too.
"Oh no... I'll be late."
Shaking off my thoughts, I headed into the hospital. I made my way to the staff elevator just to the right of the entrance and pressed the call button.

Adjacent to Saeki Hospital was a care and welfare center for the elderly. The residents there underwent rehabilitation and treatment in a state-of-the-art facility, with the goal of returning to normal life at home.
In addition to doctors and nurses, the center employed people who specialized in caregiving in a supplementary role.
Since there wasn't yet an official certification in Japan, I'd heard they went by various titles, but in my orientation course here, they were called 'caregivers.'
And the part-time job I'd been doing since just before spring break this year, thanks to Chisato's introduction, was assisting those caregivers.
"——Sorry I'm late!"
I rushed into the hospital's changing room, threw on my uniform, and hurried into the caregivers' break room, still slightly out of breath.
I hadn't actually run — this was a medical facility, after all — but the tension of arriving just barely in time had left beads of sweat on my forehead.
"My, my, thank you for coming.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1002. You're not late at all, you know.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1003. You're right on time."
An older caregiver said that with a gentle smile, pointing at the clock on the wall.
...She was right, technically, but one of my unofficial duties was to boil tea for the staff who were finally getting a break, and that was supposed to be done ahead of time.
They told me it wasn't an obligation, but with my limited abilities, letting even that slide felt like I was neglecting the work I'd been given, and that was inexcusable.
"You're a student, Natsumi-chan, and it's quite a commute for you, isn't it?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1005. So it's perfectly fine if you're a little late."
"Thank you, Yamase-san.@k@vS04/36/310100212. ...But still."
"——Oh, that's right.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1007. Natsumi-chan?"
Perhaps sensing my unease, my supervisor — the head caregiver, Yamase-san — clapped her hands together as if remembering something.
Still wearing her calm smile, she picked up a file from the table, turned to face me, and spoke.

"I'm sorry to ask right when you've arrived,@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1009. but it's almost time for the three o'clock snack, so could you help with the serving?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1010. One of our regular staff had an emergency and isn't here today."
"Oh... yes, of course!"
I nodded briskly. She was a little older than my mom, but ever since I started working here, Yamase-san had always looked out for me with this kind of warmth, and I was very fond of her.
"All right everyone, it's snack time~!@k@vS04/36/310100215. Please gather in the living room and take your seats!"
After I finished setting out the trays, I made my rounds, peeking into each bedroom and calling out to the residents as brightly and gently as I could.
At this center, three to four elderly residents typically shared a room, separated by gender.
In the rooms, which were more spacious than hospital wards, groups of friends would chat and laugh together, watch the TV someone had brought in, or lie resting on their beds.
"Oh, Natsumi-chan's here today!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMa1003. It's been a while~!"
"Come to think of it, today is Saturday, isn't it?"
The moment the residents saw my face, they gave me warm, friendly smiles.

...I'd been nervous at first about whether I could do the work properly, but having been a grandma's girl ever since I was little, I was able to settle in surprisingly quickly. By now, I got along well with everyone.
"Ahaha, hello, everyone.@k@vS04/36/310100217. ...Oh, just a moment."
I went to the side of a resident who had just sat up in bed and helped them either stand or get into their wheelchair.
The work required a fair amount of strength and attentiveness, but hearing 'thank you' when I did it properly always made me happy.
"Natsumi-chan really is such a good girl.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMb1003. So earnest and kind~."
"Absolutely.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMa1004. You don't see that in young people these days."
"N-no, that's not true at all.@k@vS04/36/310100219. ...I'm really clumsy, so please don't hesitate to tell me if I'm missing anything."
"Nonsense!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMc1002. What matters is whether you put your heart into it.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMc1003. I wish I could teach my daughter-in-law a thing or two from you!"
The old men said that as their wrinkled faces crinkled up with laughter. Their gentle expressions were so warm that I couldn't help but feel my own cheeks relax into a smile.
"That's right~!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMd1002. Say, Natsumi-chan, would you like to marry my son?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMd1003. We'd welcome you with open arms!"
"Wh-whaaaat?!"
"Now, now, Mitsu-san!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMb1005. Didn't we all agree there'd be no jumping the queue?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMb1006. And isn't your son turning past fifty this year?"
"Ohhh, that's right.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMd1005. I completely forgot~!"
""Gahahahaha!!!""

What hearty laughter. Maybe it was flattery, or maybe I was just flattering myself, but... simply being praised with such warmth always lifted my spirits. That alone made me grateful I'd taken this job.
changeReason: OLD says "I was really good at this job"; JPN and NEW say she was glad she took the job -- different sentiments.

TextENGNew: "All right then, let's head to the living room.@k@vS04/36/310100222. Sir, are you okay with the wheelchair?"
"Of course!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMb1009. It moves like an extension of my own body now.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMb1010. Maybe I'll whip myself into shape and enter a competition~."
"Really?@k@vS04/36/310100224. Then I'll make a packed lunch and come cheer you on~.@k@vS04/36/310100225. Ahaha!"
"Gahahaha!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMb1012. I'll hold you to that~!"
Taizou-san flexed the bicep on his thin arm.
...When he'd first been admitted, the weakening of his legs had left him completely dejected and listless. But now he'd brightened up entirely, becoming someone who kept the whole room entertained.
changeReason: OLD misassigns to first person ("I wasn't feeling well"); JPN describes the old man's condition when first admitted.

TextENGNew: Seeing people around my grandmother's age regain their energy like this filled my heart with genuine joy... and happiness.
"All right everyone, take the seat marked with your name tag, like always.@k@vS04/36/310100227. Everyone's getting the same portion, so no fighting, okay?"
"Ha ha ha!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMa1007. Grudges over food are a fearsome thing~.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMa1008. Better watch out, Jirokichi-san."
"Oh come now, Tome-san — my argument with Taizou-san was about a controversial call in the sumo match!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_elderMc1006. I would never stoop to quarreling over something as petty as food!"
changeReason: OLD misinterprets 相撲の物言い (a disputed sumo call) as "more like sumo wrestling."

TextENGNew: "Oh, isn't it about time for the broadcast to start?"
"I'd rather watch the drama reruns~."
Bantering back and forth like that, everyone filed noisily out of the room. After seeing them off, I quickly tidied the area around the beds and headed to the next room.
"All right, this is the last one.@k@vS04/36/310100229. ...Hm?"
I'd finished making my rounds, and when I peeked into the last room at the end of the hallway...
There, all alone, sat an old man perched on the edge of his bed with his back to me, gazing out the window.
"Sir?@k@vS04/36/310100231. It's time for your snack~."
I called out from the doorway, but... there was no reply. Thinking he might not have heard me, I stepped into the room. I approached from the side, careful not to startle him by shouting from behind.
I quietly peered at him from the side. ...An unfamiliar face. Come to think of it, Yamase-san had mentioned a few days ago that someone new had been admitted.
'He's a bit... unusual, that old man.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1012. He doesn't seem to be good with people, and we just can't get him to open up to anyone...'
The old man didn't so much as glance my way. There was no way he hadn't noticed me, and yet... he simply sat there, staring vacantly outside.
There was no life in his expression.

...For a moment I held my breath, wondering if he might be unwell, but then I noticed him blinking and let out a quiet sigh of relief. Once more, gently and slowly, I called out to him, taking care not to startle him.
Looking closely, I could see his lips moving faintly, murmuring something. It felt a little intrusive, but I steeled myself and leaned just a fraction closer, straining to hear. And then—
"......Oyashiro-sama...@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1004. ...for...give..."
"...Oyashiro... sama...?"
It was faint... but the word I heard unmistakably snagged on something in my memory.
'Oyashiro-sama'...
Could it be... was it possible...?
"...Um, sir...@k@vS04/36/310100238. ...are you by any chance from Hinamizawa?"

Without any warning, the old man whipped around to face me. The shock sent me stumbling backward, and I landed hard on my backside. Wincing and rubbing my hip, I looked up to find him staring down at me with wide, astonished eyes.
Now that I got a proper look, he had a rather stern face. ...Had I asked about something I shouldn't have?
changeReason: OLD translates 強面 (stern-faced) as "moderately strong," changing appearance to physical capability.

TextENGNew: "...I-I'm sorry!@k@vS04/36/310100242. I shouldn't have asked something so strange, I——"
"Young lady.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1007. ...Are you a child of Hinamizawa?"
Still bewildered, I gulped and nodded. ...Technically, I was from neighboring Okinomiya, but my grandmother was originally from Hinamizawa village, so it wasn't exactly a lie.
"...What's your name?"
"Uh...@k@vS04/36/310100246. Natsumi... Kimiyoshi."
"Kimiyoshi?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1010. ——Then, of the Three Families?"
"Oh, yes.@k@vS04/36/310100248. That's right."
As I answered, my suspicion solidified into certainty. Hearing the name 'Kimiyoshi' and immediately connecting it to the 'Three Families' — only someone from Hinamizawa would know that.
And it seemed the old man felt the same way. The hollow, lifeless look in his eyes suddenly gave way to a spark of light, and his stony expression broke wide open.
"...Oh, ohhh!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1012. To meet a fellow villager's child in a place like this!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1013. What an incredible coincidence~!"

I was startled when he suddenly grabbed my hand, and then even more startled when he squeezed it with a force that belied his appearance — or rather, was entirely in keeping with it — making me yelp in pain.
He let go as soon as he saw my reaction, but... my palm was bright red. Sniffle.

"Oh, sorry about that.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1015. No, it's just — I'm so happy~.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1015_1. I was feeling so blue, cooped up in a place like this in the city, but what a wonderful surprise this is!@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1015_2. Perhaps this too is Oyashiro-sama's guidance~.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1015_3. Wahahaha!!"
"A-ahaha..."
I gave a stiff laugh and an uncertain nod. It was flattering to see him so overjoyed, but more than that, I was taken aback by the complete transformation in his demeanor from just moments ago.
What on earth had that gravely somber air been about...?

...For a while after that, I listened as the old man shared stories of his memories of Hinamizawa. He had seemed difficult to approach at first, but once he started talking, he turned out to be quite the raconteur.
"So your name was Natsumi-chan, was it?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1017. When did you come to the city?"
"Me?@k@vS04/36/310100253. Um, last year.@k@vS04/36/310100254. What about you?"
"I came this spring.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1019. My son and his wife are both working now, so I had no choice but to come here."
"...I see."

"Well, living in the city is expensive, and it's hard to get by on my son's income alone.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1021. My legs have been getting weaker lately, too. I can't go burdening the young folks."
He shrugged with a bitter smile, but let out a sigh tinged with loneliness. ...I noticed the name 'Hatakeyama' written on the breast of his pajamas.
"Cheer up, sir.@k@vS04/36/310100257. If you work hard and get your strength back, I'm sure you'll be able to live with your son and his wife again!"
"......You think so?"

"Of course!@k@vS04/36/310100259. There have been plenty of people here who started out bedridden and could barely move, but worked hard at their rehabilitation until they could walk again and went home.@k@vS04/36/310100260. So you can do it too, okay?"
"...Thank you, Natsumi-chan.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1024. You're a good girl~."
Old man Hatakeyama said that with a warm, happy smile. Seeing it, I smiled and nodded right along with him.
Just then, I heard Yamase-san's voice calling, 'Natsumi-chan~!' ...Which meant everyone except the two of us must have already gathered in the living room.
"...Sir?@k@vS04/36/310100262. It's time for the three o'clock snack, so shall we go together?@k@vS04/36/310100263. How about it?"
"Hm?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_hatakeG1026. ...Oh, no, I'm not really fond of sweet things..."
"Come on~!@k@vS04/36/310100265. You've only just arrived here, so you need to get to know everyone.@k@vS04/36/310100266. Think of it as the first step in your rehabilitation, okay?"
His stern, furrowed brow was a little intimidating, but... feeling a certain kinship as fellow villagers, I beamed at him with a warm, friendly smile.
Seeing that, the old man reluctantly hauled himself to his feet with a quiet grunt.
His footing was steadier than I'd expected, so I only lightly rested my hand against his. Even so, the hand that held mine was warm, and something about its gentleness made me happy.
"...By the way, sir?"
"What is it?"
"Um, earlier——"
Why were you murmuring 'Oyashiro-sama'?
I was about to ask that, but in that instant my grandmother's face flashed through my mind, and I barely managed to hold my tongue.
I didn't really believe in Oyashiro-sama. None of the kids my age back in Okinomiya did.
But at the same time, I knew deep in my bones from experience that for the village elders, any joke or offhand remark about Oyashiro-sama — let alone denying the existence — was an absolute taboo.
"...What's the matter?"
"No, it's nothing.@k@vS04/36/310100270. Come on, let's go."
But... a small, prickling unease lingered in my chest, like the point of a needle...
At the care center, bedtime came immediately after dinner. After checking on every room and finishing the cleaning in the living room, my shift was over.
"Good work today."
"Good work.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1014. ...Thank you for today, Natsumi-chan."
When I returned to the break room, Yamase-san greeted me with a warm smile and offered me a cup of hot cocoa. I accepted it gratefully, tilting my head slightly — 'Huh...?'
Did they even have cocoa in the pantry here...?
"*Giggle*@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1016. ...This is your reward, Natsumi-chan.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1017. For looking after Hatakeyama-san."
"Huh?@k@vS04/36/310100273. Oh, it was nothing..."
Cheeks loosening at the sweet aroma, I bashfully lifted the cup to my lips. ...It was sweet, and absolutely delicious.
...When I'd brought old man Hatakeyama to the living room, not only the other residents but even Yamase-san and the caregivers had been astonished.
I learned later that several people had tried reaching out to him before, but he'd completely ignored every one of them.
They'd been reluctantly bringing his meals to his room, and things had gotten so dire they'd even discussed transferring him to a different floor.
But today, he had come to dinner and eaten his entire meal. ...Knowing that made me feel just a little bit proud.
"So what kind of magic did you use?@k@vS04/00/VT1B_yamase1019. Getting Hatakeyama-san to come around like that."
"Ehehe.@k@vS04/36/310100275. ...It's a secret."
My answer came out vague. It's because we're from the same village. ...That was what I started to say, but I swallowed the words.

Partly it was embarrassment. But more than that, I felt a certain reluctance to say aloud that I came from a rural backwater called Hinamizawa — a place Yamase-san and the others had surely never heard of.
By the time I changed and stepped outside, the sun had sunk below the horizon and the sky was stained deep with the colors of dusk. A faint glimmer of stars had appeared in the western sky.
"Haah.@k@vS04/36/310100277. ...I guess it got a little late."
I glanced at my watch and let out a small sigh. ...I'd told Mom I was at a club activity, but I wondered if she'd scold me for being out this late.
The truth was... my family didn't know about my part-time job at the care center.
Part-time jobs were against school rules, of course, but that wasn't the only reason. I wanted to surprise Mom and Dad with a wedding anniversary present.
"They said this year is their twentieth anniversary, after all..."
Less than a month remained until the day. Dad was oblivious to that sort of thing, so he'd surely let it pass without a thought.
But despite how she seemed, Mom was surprisingly romantic. She wouldn't say it outright, but she was definitely hoping for something to mark the occasion.
"So the daughter will step in for her clueless father...@k@vS04/36/310100280. Just kidding."
Murmuring that, I pictured Dad's face and couldn't help but grin. He'd be mortified by his own obliviousness, but I was sure he'd clasp his hands together and thank me profusely.
changeReason: OLD says "embarrassed by my uncouth behavior"; JPN says he'd be embarrassed by his own boorishness -- different subject.

TextENGNew: Not that I was keeping score. No matter how it came about, all I needed was for everyone in the family to be smiling and in good spirits. That alone was enough. That was my happiness.
"...Besides."
That wasn't my only motivation. ...The other reason was that by working a part-time job, I wanted to change the person I was in this city.
There was something I'd been thinking about ever since my days in Okinomiya. It started when I happened to see a girl from the Sonozaki main family — Mion — at work...
Always cheerful, quick and capable, and somehow possessing a maturity beyond her years despite being younger than me — a girl of real substance. That was Mion Sonozaki.
She always took the lead as a matter of course. I was sure that someone like Mion could breeze through any adversity.
And unlike the rest of us, she always spoke with adults and elders on equal footing.
There was something cool about that... and honestly, though it was only a small prickle, I did feel something close to a competitive spark.
Even I wouldn't be a child forever. I'd grown. I'd worked hard. So surely I could be entrusted with something — could be of use to someone.
And yet in Okinomiya, I'd been kept within the boundaries of childhood, and no one had let me prove otherwise.
changeReason: OLD says "I couldn't forgive"; JPN says her claims weren't accepted by others -- reversed agency.

TextENGNew: But in this city, surely——
So... maybe it sounded childish, but a 'part-time job' was a symbol — one small step on the path toward adulthood.
It might have been something very small and fragile. But right now, anything would do. I wanted something tangible — some proof I could hold onto.
The confidence that I was allowed to be in this city, and a clear, definite place where I belonged——
That said, Mom would probably still be angry if she found out I was breaking school rules... But even though I'd started this on impulse, I couldn't bring myself to quit halfway through.
And besides......
Just then.
"————Kimiyoshi...?"
I had just stepped through the hospital's service gate when I looked at the face of a boy passing by on the street, and... froze. Because it was——
Yes... it was dim and I could barely make him out, but there was no mistaking it — it was Akira Toudou.
"Wh... what are you doing here?"
"Oh, I was just... on my way back from sketching."
I managed an awkward laugh, but cold sweat was trickling down my back.
Hospital visiting hours were long over. And on top of that, this was the service entrance — complete with a staff-only sign helpfully illuminated by a spotlight.
The only people who came and went through here were hospital personnel and staff from the adjacent care center.
Wh-what should I do...?
If Toudou-kun found out I was working a part-time job, it could get back to the school. No — that wasn't all. Would he look down on me for being the kind of student who broke the rules...?
"......Kimiyoshi?"
"Oh, um, that is..."
There was no helping it.
I hastily concocted an excuse — 'I used the service entrance since visiting hours were over' — and though I felt guilty, I was about to open my mouth to explain when——
"Well, Kimiyoshi-san.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_kaigoFa1002. Great work taking care of Hatakeyama-san today.@k@vS04/00/VT1B_kaigoFa1003. Be careful on your way home, okay?"
A senior caregiver emerged from the service entrance, greeted me with those words, and walked off in the other direction.
...What spectacularly awful timing.
